title: rudbeckia laciniata subsp. ampla
intro: Rudbeckia laciniata subsp. ampla, also known as the cutleaf coneflower, is a perennial plant that belongs to the sunflower family (Asteraceae). It is native to the southeastern United States and has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. It can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is also used in topical applications for skin issues and wounds.
